sanskrit: यातयामं गतरसं पूति पर्युषितं च यत् | उच्छिष्टमपि चामेध्यं भोजनं तामसप्रियम् ||
transliteration: yāta-yāmaṁ gata-rasaṁ pūti paryuṣitaṁ ca yat | ucchiṣṭam api cāmedhyaṁ bhojanaṁ tāmasa-priyam ||
literal_meaning: Food that is stale, tasteless, putrid, leftover, impure, is dear to those in tamas.
contextual_meaning: Dull, lifeless, or contaminated food mirrors and reinforces a tamasic mind—lethargic, hopeless, and cut off from vitality.
anchor_text: Stale food feeds depression; choose fresh vitality.
psychological_state: Tamasic depression marked by low energy, hopelessness, and self-disgust.
trigger_scenario: Client bingeing on spoiled take-out after three days in bed, saying "I don’t care."
